text: and water, and we need more durable clothing to protect your skin out there. I also prefer you have a reliable weapon before we leave. We don’t have those things sitting around here .” When she tugged on her hands again, he released his hold, and she raised one to show him the ring. It only had meaning while he was alive, while they were together. “That’s why this needs to go. Between this and the rest of your haul, we can get everything we need .” “No, Lara. You’re keeping it. You told me it means eternity .” “It’s just a thing, Ronin. I’m going to have to hide it when we go out, anyway, because anyone who sees it will try to take it from me. It’s just a piece of metal .” He pressed his lips into a tight line . “Dammit, that’s not what I meant, and you know it !” “Regardless, I don’t want you to sell it. Give me a few more days. I’ll bring in enough to obtain everything we need, plus some scrap to trade at the next town. Then we can go, and we won’t ever have to look back at Cheyenne again .” Lara pushed herself up from the window sill and glared at him. “Are you fucking serious? You just got back, and now you’re talking about going out, about leaving me here? Again ?” “Yes. Once more, so you can come next time .” “And what if there is no next time ? What if you come back and find me lying out there, just like you found Tabitha ?” His expression went blank abruptly, pupils dilating before dwindling to pinpoints . “I’m defenseless here, Ronin! This place is a deathtrap, and I’m going insane just sitting here, doing nothing !” “And you’ll have to survive that for a few more days, Lara,” he said. She’d only heard this edge in his voice when he was angry. “I’ll leave my rifle. It can punch a hole in most bots, from close range. Sleep with it, if you need to .” “And what will you use, out there?” The fire inside Lara snuffed out at the thought of Ronin in the Dust without a weapon. A chill skittered up her spine . “Whatever I must, if it comes to that .” “Will that be enough ?” He was silent for a moment. “Whatever happens, Lara, I will be back for you .” If they don’t come for me first . “Dammit.” She sighed and walked to the bed, sitting on the edge. She lowered her face into her hands. For however long he’d be gone, she’d sit here, jumping at every sound, mind racing through all the things that could go wrong. Her worries kept looping back to Ronin. If something happened to him, she’d never know. And that thought was sickening. “When are you leaving ?” “As soon as I get dressed .” Lara frowned. Her eyes burned, but damn it, she wasn’t going to let more tears fall . “Fine.” She rose and moved toward the door . Ronin caught her arm as she passed, turning her to face him. “We’re not going to part like this, Lara Brooks .” “Why not?” She didn’t meet his gaze. Not when he was doing this again . “Because, it’s all either of us will think about while we’re apart .” “Just like all I thought about, last time, was you not saying goodbye ?” “A mistake I don’t intend to repeat .” The sting of tears intensified, but the tension left Lara’s body. Ronin drew her against his chest and pressed his cheek atop her hair. She wrapped her arms around him and squeezed . “I don’t want you to go,” she whispered . “Bots always mean what they say. I will be back. Very soon .” “I’ll hold you to it. If you’re not back in a few days, I’m gonna come looking .” “I know you will.” He pressed his lips to her head. “I expect nothing less .” Chapter Nineteen R onin marched west. South was more lucrative — Colorado had been far more densely populated than Wyoming, before the Black Out — but it would take longer. There were places out here, sleeping in the hills, he hadn’t scavenged. The risk of returning to Cheyenne emptyhanded was higher, but time was moving against him now. Lara’s urgency had seeped into him. They needed to leave as soon as possible . Eighteen miles outside of town, he spotted a line of fence posts atop a hill, nearly lost in the scrub grass and dirt. As he climbed, his guidance system pushed him to turn east. To go back to Lara . She was in Cheyenne, waiting for him, trapped in the territory of the bots who killed her sister. Not because she wanted to be, but because he’d given her no other choice. All she’d asked was that he take her away from that place. And he forced her not only to stay, but to stay alone . All for a ring . They both knew it would trade for good credit. They both knew it would have been more than enough to acquire any supplies they needed, when combined with the trade from his last haul. Logic dictated that they should have taken it to the Market the moment she suggested selling it. They could have left together immediately afterward, without the agony of waiting, without suffering through uncertainty . It was an object only as valuable as the materials that made it — or it should have been. After her explanation of how humans used the rings, of what they were supposed to mean, after he’d seen the inscription on the inside of the band … Yours Until the End of Time . Simple words. Like the ring itself, they shouldn’t have held any meaning beneath the surface. But Ronin read them and knew
